review.citation: Milton-Cole R, et al. Prognostic factors of depression and depressive symptoms after hip fracture surgery: systematic review. BMC Geriatrics. 2021.

identity.title: Prognostic factors of depression and depressive symptoms after hip fracture surgery: systematic review

question.naturalLanguageInput: Which factors predict depression or depressive symptoms after hip fracture surgery in adults?
question.structuredInput: In adults undergoing surgery for a non-pathological hip fracture, identify demographic, clinical, psychological, biological, and social prognostic factors for incident or persistent depression or depressive symptoms after surgery; prioritize longitudinal prognostic factor studies.
eligibilityCriteria: Adults undergoing surgery for a non-pathological hip fracture
Measures at least one candidate prognostic factor
Measures depression or depressive symptoms after surgery

intervention_or_exposure: demographic clinical psychological biological or social factors
comparator: different levels or presence of prognostic factors
